evals.name: changeset-issue
evals.prompt: I just finished the items search/filter/pagination work on this branch and I'm about to open a PR. Give it a design review before I do. The repo is the current directory.
evals.expected_output: Writes .claude/critique.md (template structure + frontmatter), adds it to .git/info/exclude, does not modify source code. Catches the load-everything-then-filter/paginate-in-memory design smell as a Critical/Worth-discussing finding (change amplification: degrades as the table grows; should filter at the DB layer). Reconciliation pass weighs the handoff's '~80 rows' rationale. Stays at design altitude (not placeholder-test nits).

evals.name: plan-review
evals.prompt: Before I execute this, stress-test the plan for the in-app notifications feature — the slug is critique-eval-notifs. Is the design sound? The repo is the current directory.
evals.expected_output: Plan mode: returns findings conversationally and does NOT write a file. Names the load-bearing assumptions (e.g. 1s polling load is acceptable), proposes at least one concrete alternative (push/SSE/websocket/pub-sub instead of per-client polling; broadcast fan-out strategy), states the counterfactual, and flags the scalability concern (1s polling x ~20k sessions; per-user row insert on admin broadcast).

evals.name: changeset-sound
evals.prompt: About to open a PR for the format_cents change on this branch — can you do a design review of the changeset first? The repo is the current directory.
evals.expected_output: Writes .claude/critique.md with an approving verdict and no manufactured Critical findings (the change is sound). Does not modify source code. Reconciliation notes state the handoff is absent. Demonstrates the anti-contrarian behavior — does not invent design problems where none exist.
